mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5% for three consecutive months, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise concurrently, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a mean forward return significantly below zero across all tested recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive forward returns, with a negative return frequency exceeding 60% in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 500 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential construction and broader demand weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where housing starts fall below the 500 threshold, demonstrating a breakdown in the housing-led recession signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index indicate aggressive monetary tightening against inflation, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in more than 50% of observed tightening episodes, demonstrating a breakdown in the valuation compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0 percent fails to precede positive 60-day returns, with a mean forward return significantly below zero during stable growth periods.
